is_p_falciparum.evidence: Study targets P. falciparum kelch 13 and pfmdr1; enrolled patients had P. falciparum mono-infections confirmed by PET-PCR at species level.

reports_target_markers.evidence: Reports pfmdr1 codon 86 (N86Y) — a curated target marker. k13 propeller domain codons 430-726 also sequenced but only R622I (validated) found; pfmdr1 86 is explicitly tabulated.

reports_extractable_frequencies.evidence: Table 3 gives per-region per-year counts and percentages for N86 (e.g., Kigoma 2016: 118 (100.0%)); Table 2 gives k13 mutant counts per region/year.

sub_country_location.evidence: Frequencies reported by region and sentinel site (Kigoma, Morogoro, Pwani, Tanga, Mbeya, Mtwara, Tabora, Mwanza) with named health centres/sites (Igombe, Ipinda, Yombo, Mkuzi, Mlimba, Nagaga, Simbo, Ujiji).

temporal_resolution_within_3y.evidence: Tables 2 and 3 report data per single collection year (2016, 2018, 2019, 2020, 2021), well within 3-year windows.

african_field_samples.evidence: DBS samples collected from enrolled malaria patients at TES sentinel sites in Mainland Tanzania, an African country.

is_primary_study.evidence: Primary empirical study analyzing molecularly genotyped field samples collected in TES 2016-2021.

duplicate_risk.evidence: Samples come from TES conducted by NMCP; '2016 study has been published [22], 2018 and 2019 studies are available online [25, 26]'; part of PARMA network. Data may overlap with prior TES publications and country-wide surveillance.

supplement.evidence: Per-region, per-year frequency counts and percentages for N86 (pfmdr1) and k13 mutations are given directly in Tables 2 and 3 in the main text.

assessment.summary: Retrospective analysis of 2,015 P. falciparum DBS samples from TES at eight NMCP sentinel sites across Mainland Tanzania (2016-2021), genotyping k13 and pfmdr1. Reports extractable per-region, per-year frequencies for pfmdr1 N86 (target marker) plus Y184F, D1246Y and k13 mutations. Sub-national resolution and single-year temporal binning satisfy all criteria. Duplicate risk high because these are TES/PARMA samples with several years already published elsewhere.
